title: Job Vacancies at Zenith Precision Limited
content:
Zenith Precision Limited (RC713781) is a technical service provider since 2007; we provide complete solution in coding and marking, project management consultancy and engineering procurement services for plastic, bottling, pharmaceutical and processing plants. The company has a team of seasoned professionals with years of working experience and we have successfully delivered various projects in various manufacturing industries.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Senior HR Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking a proactive and experienced Senior HR Officer to oversee and drive our HR operations.
	This role is ideal for a results-oriented professional looking to grow into a leadership position while making a measurable impact in a fast-growing organization.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage recruitment. onboarding and OffbOMding processes
	Maintain and accurate eCOrdS and HP Sy Stems
	Drive perfomanCe mmagement and development initiatives
	Coordinate and benefits administration
	EnSwe with Nigerian labor Ccnpany
	Handle relations. Ccmflict and discCinary processes
	Support the and of HR poEcies procedure
	Lead HR initiatives that improve efficiency engagement. and overall business performance


Qualifications & Experience


	Minimum of 5 years of ooerience in Human Resources or a related roe
	Strong of practices, Labor and systems
	Proven ability to manage HR operations effectively
	Communicati«n and organizational
	Proficiency in HR software and Microsoft Office Suite.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Senior Account Officer

Job Location: Lagos

Job Summary


	The Senior Account Officer is responsible for overseeing financial operations managing accounting records, ensuring compliance with regulatory standards, and providing startegic financial insights to support business decisions.
	This role requires strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and ability to supervise junior accounting staff


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare, review, & analyze financial statements and reports
	Manage general ledger activities, including reconcillations and journal entries Oversee accounts payable & receivable functions
	Ensure compliance with financial regulations, tax laws, and internal policies Assist in budgeting, forecasting, & financial planning
	Monitor cash flow and financial planning
	Coordinate audits and recommend mitigation strategies.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field
	5-8 years of relevant accounting experience
	Professional certification (e.g, ACA, ACCA, CPA) is highly preferred
	Strong knowledge of accounting principles and financial reporting
	Proficiency in accounting software an Microsoft Excel
	Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
	Strong leadership and team management abilities
	High level of integrity and attention to detail.


Skills & Competencies:


	Financial analysis and reporting
	Leaadership and supervisors
	Communication and interpersonal skills Time management and organization
	Regulatory compliance knnowledge.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.


 

3.) Field Technician

Job Location: Aba, Abia

Job Description


	The Field Technician is responsible for installing. maintaining, and repairing technical equipment and systems across multiple locations.
	The role requires hands-Zin technical expertise, strong problem-solving skills, and the ability to work independently while supporting operational efficiency.


Key Responsibilities


	Install. inspect. maintain, and repair equipment and technical Systerns at assigned
	Perform routine preventive maintenance to ensure optimal system performance
	Troubleshoot and resolve technical faults in a timely manner
	Maintain accurate service records, reports. and documentation Of all technical activities
	Ensure compliance with safety standards and procedures
	Provide on-site technical support and guidance to or where necessary


Requirements


	Bachelor's in Electrical Or a related field
	Proven experience as a technician or in a similar technical role
	Strong of tools , and troubleshooting techniques
	Ablity to read and interpet and drawirvs
	Wülingness to travel frequently between assigned locatbns


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidate should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Job Openings at Obasanjo Farms Nigeria
content:
Obasanjo Farms Nigeria offers some of the best foods and poultry products in the market. For our fellow partners in the agriculture industry, we can help to provide tools, machinery and services to help grow your agri-business. Our priority is to provide the best food without compromise. We’re a progressive food company raising the expectations for how much good food can do. Come, see what makes Obasanjo Farms Nigeria a great company.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Chief Security Officer

Job Location: Ogun

Requirements
     

            
    




	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.

 

2.) Senior Auditor

Job Location: Ogun

Requirements


	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Procurement Manager

Job Location: Ogun

Requirements


	Candidates should possess relevant qualifications and experience.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and Cover Letter to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Station Manager",
  "description": "Fatgbems Group is a diversified Nigerian conglomerate with interests in petroleum marketing, gas services, automotive solutions, agriculture, and real estate. With over 30 years of industry experience, we are committed to delivering quality services, operational excellence, innovation, and sustainable value to our customers and stakeholders. We offer a dynamic work environment that fosters growth, professionalism, and career development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Station Manager

Job Location: Amuwo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Station Manager is responsible for the overall management and profitability of the retail filling station.
	The role ensures efficient day-to-day operations, excellent customer service, strict compliance with health, safety, security, and environmental (HSSE) standards, inventory control, financial accountability, and effective management of station personnel.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Oversee the daily operations of the filling station.
	Ensure uninterrupted availability of petroleum products and lubricants.
	Monitor fuel dispensing activities to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
	Ensure all station equipment, pumps, generators, and facilities are in good working condition.
	Coordinate maintenance and promptly report equipment faults.


Sales and Business Development:


	Drive sales of fuel, lubricants, LPG, and convenience store products.
	Implement marketing initiatives to increase customer traffic and revenue.
	Monitor daily sales performance against targets.
	Identify opportunities to improve profitability and reduce operational costs.


Inventory Management:


	Monitor stock levels of petroleum products, lubricants, and other inventory.
	Conduct daily tank dip measurements and reconcile stock.
	Ensure accurate stock receipt, storage, and issuance.
	Investigate and report stock variances or product losses.


Financial Management:


	Manage daily cash collections and banking.
	Prepare daily sales and financial reports.
	Reconcile cash, POS transactions, transfers, and credit sales.
	Ensure proper documentation and accountability of all financial transactions.
	Minimize revenue leakage and financial risks.


Customer Service:


	Maintain high standards of customer service.
	Resolve customer complaints promptly and professionally.
	Ensure customers receive quality products and efficient service.
	
	Promote customer loyalty and satisfaction.
	


Health, Safety, Security, and Environment (HSSE):


	Enforce compliance with all HSSE policies and regulatory requirements.
	Conduct regular safety inspections and emergency drills.
	Ensure fire-fighting equipment is available and functional.
	Prevent environmental pollution and unsafe practices.
	Investigate and report accidents, incidents, and near misses.


Staff Management:


	Supervise cashiers, pump attendants, security personnel, and other station staff.
	Prepare staff schedules and duty rosters.
	Train, coach, and mentor employees.
	Conduct performance evaluations and provide feedback.
	Enforce discipline and company policies.


Compliance and Reporting:


	Ensure compliance with company policies and government regulations.
	Maintain accurate operational records and documentation.
	Submit daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports.
	Prepare reports on sales, inventory, maintenance, and staffing.


Facility Management:


	Ensure station premises are clean, safe, and well-maintained.
	Monitor utility usage and operational costs.
	Coordinate repairs and maintenance of station facilities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Science,Geology,Business Administration, Management, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field.
	Professional certifications in Retail Management, Health &amp; Safety, or Operations Management are an added advantage.
	Must Reside within Lagos and its environs.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: humanresources@fatgbemsgroup.com using the Job Position as subject of the email.",

Station Manager Job at Fatgbems Group




        
    

        
        Fatgbems Group is a diversified Nigerian conglomerate with interests in petroleum marketing, gas services, automotive solutions, agriculture, and real estate. With over 30 years of industry experience, we are committed to delivering quality services, operational excellence, innovation, and sustainable value to our customers and stakeholders. We offer a dynamic work environment that fosters growth, professionalism, and career development.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Station Manager

Job Location: Amuwo, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Station Manager is responsible for the overall management and profitability of the retail filling station.
	The role ensures efficient day-to-day operations, excellent customer service, strict compliance with health, safety, security, and environmental (HSSE) standards, inventory control, financial accountability, and effective management of station personnel.


Key Responsibilities
Operations Management:


	Oversee the daily operations of the filling station.
	Ensure uninterrupted availability of petroleum products and lubricants.
	Monitor fuel dispensing activities to ensure accuracy and efficiency.
	Ensure all station equipment, pumps, generators, and facilities are in good working condition.
	Coordinate maintenance and promptly report equipment faults.


Sales and Business Development:


	Drive sales of fuel, lubricants, LPG, and convenience store products.
	Implement marketing initiatives to increase customer traffic and revenue.
	Monitor daily sales performance against targets.
	Identify opportunities to improve profitability and reduce operational costs.


Inventory Management:


	Monitor stock levels of petroleum products, lubricants, and other inventory.
	Conduct daily tank dip measurements and reconcile stock.
	Ensure accurate stock receipt, storage, and issuance.
	Investigate and report stock variances or product losses.


Financial Management:


	Manage daily cash collections and banking.
	Prepare daily sales and financial reports.
	Reconcile cash, POS transactions, transfers, and credit sales.
	Ensure proper documentation and accountability of all financial transactions.
	Minimize revenue leakage and financial risks.


Customer Service:


	Maintain high standards of customer service.
	Resolve customer complaints promptly and professionally.
	Ensure customers receive quality products and efficient service.
	
	Promote customer loyalty and satisfaction.
	


Health, Safety, Security, and Environment (HSSE):


	Enforce compliance with all HSSE policies and regulatory requirements.
	Conduct regular safety inspections and emergency drills.
	Ensure fire-fighting equipment is available and functional.
	Prevent environmental pollution and unsafe practices.
	Investigate and report accidents, incidents, and near misses.


Staff Management:


	Supervise cashiers, pump attendants, security personnel, and other station staff.
	Prepare staff schedules and duty rosters.
	Train, coach, and mentor employees.
	Conduct performance evaluations and provide feedback.
	Enforce discipline and company policies.


Compliance and Reporting:


	Ensure compliance with company policies and government regulations.
	Maintain accurate operational records and documentation.
	Submit daily, weekly, and monthly operational reports.
	Prepare reports on sales, inventory, maintenance, and staffing.


Facility Management:


	Ensure station premises are clean, safe, and well-maintained.
	Monitor utility usage and operational costs.
	Coordinate repairs and maintenance of station facilities.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Science,Geology,Business Administration, Management, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field.
	Professional certifications in Retail Management, Health & Safety, or Operations Management are an added advantage.
	Must Reside within Lagos and its environs.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Manager",
  "description": "Pruvia Integrated Limited, we are general service merchants with a strong desire to ensure customer satisfaction in the midst of efficient and effective service delivery. As an organization we are driven by the dictum of &lsquo;Impossibility is Nothing&rsquo;. PIL was conceived in 2007, as regards to its incorporation and duly registered in 2013 with Corporate Affairs Commission of Nigeria (CAC) with Registration Number RC 1097861. It originated in response to providing world class services and solutions to the society at large and offering an alternative that is satisfactory in areas of our specialty. It has carried out service rendering duties in various states of Nigeria and within the ever bustling business environment of Lagos.

Our Stream of Services ranges from Human Resource Management, (Talent Acquistion Management Recruitment, Outsourcing, Sales Outsourcing, Training &amp; Development, Employment Verification Sevices (Background Check), Outsourcing, Job Crisis Management, HR Consultancy), Fleet Management Operation &amp; Marking &amp; Business Support Services. We offer the best candidates to our clients and also offer end to end solutions in terms of Manpower development and supply. Our clients represent the entire spectrum of the Commerce, Hospitality, Information technology, Media, Finance &amp; Insurance Sector, Automobile, Oil &amp; Gas Sector, Real Estate, Manufacturing, FMCG and Small and Medium Enterprises and Communications industry. Pruvia was established by individuals who had diverse experience in professional programs in Human Resource Management, Business Management, and Marketing etc. PIL was formed with achieving and exuding corporate standards in providing solutions and quality service delivery in our core areas (Human resource management, Training, Market Activation/Campaign etc).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Ogudu / Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Sales Manager will be responsible for driving property sales, managing client relationships, and achieving revenue targets through effective marketing and negotiation strategies.
	This role involves assisting clients in buying, selling, or leasing properties by understanding their needs, analyzing market trends, and ensuring seamless transactions.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong sales acumen, market insight, and excellent communication skills, with the ability to close deals and maintain long-term client satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Client Consultation &amp; Lead Generation:


	Identify and develop potential clients through networking, referrals, and marketing campaigns.
	Guide clients through the property buying, selling, and leasing processes.
	Provide clients with expert insights on property valuations, investment opportunities, and market trends.


Marketing &amp; Property Showcasing:


	Develop and execute marketing strategies to promote available properties.
	Create and manage online listings, brochures, flyers, and other promotional materials.
	Organize, coordinate, and conduct property showings and open houses.


Negotiation &amp; Deal Closure:


	Negotiate offers, pricing, and contract terms between buyers and sellers.
	Prepare and manage transaction documents, contracts, and sales agreements.
	Liaise with relevant professionals (e.g., mortgage lenders, surveyors, inspectors, and lawyers) to ensure smooth deal closure.


Administrative &amp; Record Keeping:


	Maintain accurate records of property listings, transactions, and client communications.
	Utilize CRM systems to manage leads, follow-ups, and client databases efficiently.
	Schedule and manage appointments, meetings, and communication with clients and team members.


Required Skills &amp; Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, or a related field.
	Experience: 3&ndash;5 years in real estate sales or property management.
	Sales &amp; Negotiation: Strong persuasion, negotiation, and deal-closing abilities.
	Communication: Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills.
	Market Knowledge: In-depth understanding of the local real estate market, including property trends and valuation.
	Customer Service: Ability to build trust, provide exceptional service, and maintain long-term client relationships.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CRM tools, and digital marketing platforms.


Personal Attributes:


	Results-driven, self-motivated, and target-oriented.
	Professional appearance and demeanor.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Ability to work independently and as part of a dynamic sales team.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: jobpruvia@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted for an interview.",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Pruvia Integrated Limited"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {

Sales Manager Job at Pruvia Integrated Limited




        
    

        
        Pruvia Integrated Limited, we are general service merchants with a strong desire to ensure customer satisfaction in the midst of efficient and effective service delivery. As an organization we are driven by the dictum of ‘Impossibility is Nothing’. PIL was conceived in 2007, as regards to its incorporation and duly registered in 2013 with Corporate Affairs Commission of Nigeria (CAC) with Registration Number RC 1097861. It originated in response to providing world class services and solutions to the society at large and offering an alternative that is satisfactory in areas of our specialty. It has carried out service rendering duties in various states of Nigeria and within the ever bustling business environment of Lagos.
     

            
    



Our Stream of Services ranges from Human Resource Management, (Talent Acquistion Management Recruitment, Outsourcing, Sales Outsourcing, Training & Development, Employment Verification Sevices (Background Check), Outsourcing, Job Crisis Management, HR Consultancy), Fleet Management Operation & Marking & Business Support Services. We offer the best candidates to our clients and also offer end to end solutions in terms of Manpower development and supply. Our clients represent the entire spectrum of the Commerce, Hospitality, Information technology, Media, Finance & Insurance Sector, Automobile, Oil & Gas Sector, Real Estate, Manufacturing, FMCG and Small and Medium Enterprises and Communications industry. Pruvia was established by individuals who had diverse experience in professional programs in Human Resource Management, Business Management, and Marketing etc. PIL was formed with achieving and exuding corporate standards in providing solutions and quality service delivery in our core areas (Human resource management, Training, Market Activation/Campaign etc).

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Manager

Job Location: Ogudu / Ajah, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Sales Manager will be responsible for driving property sales, managing client relationships, and achieving revenue targets through effective marketing and negotiation strategies.
	This role involves assisting clients in buying, selling, or leasing properties by understanding their needs, analyzing market trends, and ensuring seamless transactions.
	The ideal candidate should possess strong sales acumen, market insight, and excellent communication skills, with the ability to close deals and maintain long-term client satisfaction.


Key Responsibilities
Client Consultation & Lead Generation:


	Identify and develop potential clients through networking, referrals, and marketing campaigns.
	Guide clients through the property buying, selling, and leasing processes.
	Provide clients with expert insights on property valuations, investment opportunities, and market trends.


Marketing & Property Showcasing:


	Develop and execute marketing strategies to promote available properties.
	Create and manage online listings, brochures, flyers, and other promotional materials.
	Organize, coordinate, and conduct property showings and open houses.


Negotiation & Deal Closure:


	Negotiate offers, pricing, and contract terms between buyers and sellers.
	Prepare and manage transaction documents, contracts, and sales agreements.
	Liaise with relevant professionals (e.g., mortgage lenders, surveyors, inspectors, and lawyers) to ensure smooth deal closure.


Administrative & Record Keeping:


	Maintain accurate records of property listings, transactions, and client communications.
	Utilize CRM systems to manage leads, follow-ups, and client databases efficiently.
	Schedule and manage appointments, meetings, and communication with clients and team members.


Required Skills & Qualifications


	Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, or a related field.
	Experience: 3–5 years in real estate sales or property management.
	Sales & Negotiation: Strong persuasion, negotiation, and deal-closing abilities.
	Communication: Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills.
	Market Knowledge: In-depth understanding of the local real estate market, including property trends and valuation.
	Customer Service: Ability to build trust, provide exceptional service, and maintain long-term client relationships.
	Technical Skills: Proficiency in Microsoft Office, CRM tools, and digital marketing platforms.


Personal Attributes:


	Results-driven, self-motivated, and target-oriented.
	Professional appearance and demeanor.
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Ability to work independently and as part of a dynamic sales team.


Salary
N700,000 - N800,000 monthly.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Only qualified candidates will be contacted for an interview.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Assistant Branch Manager Job at Shema Holdings Limited




        
    

        
        Shema Holdings Limited maintains close relationships with existing investment structures, funds and asset managers, and is perpetually devoting efforts to developing its networks in order to identfy appropriate investment opportunities.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant Branch Manager

Job Location: Mpape, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are looking for a proactive and result oriented Assistant Branch Manger to support in overseeing daily operations, driving business growth, and ensuring excellent customer service.


Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Assist in managing the branch's daily operations and administrative activities.
	monitor compliance with company policies, procedures and regulatory requirements.
	Assist in preparing operational and performance reports.
	Act on behalf of the Branch Manager when required.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply
